scope: AgenC agents/run-agent.ts ↔ codex codex-rs/core/src/agent/control.rs spawn_subagent + codex-rs/core/src/codex.rs run-turn loop, locked for the codex-aligned subset only. AgenC's run-agent.ts is broader than any single codex file: it bundles MCP init, child Session bootstrap, run-turn driving, and parent mailbox relay into one orchestrator. The behaviors locked here are the codex-aligned slices (cwd injection, child config building, status emission via agentStatusFromEvent, mailbox direction routing, run-turn loop termination on isFinal). The OpenClaude-shaped progress/iterator surface and AgenC-only THREAD_SPAWN_MAIN_THREAD_TOOL_NAMES exclusion set are intentional adaptations called out explicitly.

requiredBehaviors: injectChildToolArgs sets cwd from opts.worktree.path when toolName is 'system.bash' or 'exec_command' AND caller did not pass an explicit cwd string

matches codex spawn_subagent's worktree-cwd injection: when a subagent is spawned with an isolated worktree, codex's child config inherits that worktree path as the working directory for shell-shaped tools
AgenC covers both tool names because the codex-v2 shell tool was renamed from system.bash to exec_command (Phase A.1); both must continue to inherit cwd until system.bash is fully retired
write_stdin and other non-shell tools are intentionally NOT cwd-injected (write_stdin has no cwd arg in unified-exec/types.ts)
buildChildSession constructs a fresh Session with the parent's provider/model identity, the role-resolved AgentRoleConfig, and the parent's MCP/permissions wiring
matches codex spawn_subagent's child Codex bootstrap: child shares parent's session_source/provider but gets its own conversation/rollout state
buildChildConfig overrides cwd to worktree.path when present, then base.cwd otherwise (codex parity: child config takes the worktree as its working directory)
buildChildModelInfo carries reasoning effort + model name through to the child's first turn so role-supplied reasoning_effort survives
createChildRolloutStore writes the child's rollout under the parent rollout's directory, namespaced by child threadId

matches codex spawn_subagent at agent/control.rs:383-385 reading parent rollout via codex_home + thread-id resolution; AgenC writes child rollouts at the same directory level so a sibling 'find_thread_path_by_id_str' style lookup works

rollout is opt-in: when parent has no rolloutStore the child starts with an in-memory store (codex equivalent: when parent's RolloutRecorder is absent, fork falls back to in-memory parentMessages — fork-context.ts I-36 row covers this)
initMcpForAgent runs with MCP_INIT_TIMEOUT_MS=30_000 by default (I-50)
an abort signal cancels the init wait so a parent shutdown does not block on a hung MCP server
matches codex's session-bootstrap timeout on MCP server discovery: codex bounds initial connect attempts so a misbehaving MCP server cannot deadlock the run-turn loop
init failures emit a warning via emitWarning rather than throwing; the child still starts, with a degraded MCP surface
run-turn loop emits AgentStatus updates by feeding session events through agentStatusFromEvent before pushing them onto live.status

matches codex's pattern of mapping turn_started/turn_complete/turn_aborted/error events to AgentStatus variants at the agent boundary (locked separately by codex-status-parity#agent-status-from-event)
no AgenC-only status transitions are emitted from this file: every variant pushed onto live.status comes from agentStatusFromEvent or one of its named constructors
terminal status flips upInbox into the agent_exited sentinel emit window
relayToParentMailbox sends every child→parent communication with direction: 'up'
drainChildMailbox processes inbound parent→child messages with direction: 'down' and translates them into child-side input ops

matches codex mailbox.rs author/recipient routing at codex-rs/core/src/agent/mailbox.rs (the bidirectional direction tag is AgenC's adaptation; locked separately by codex-mailbox-parity#inter-agent-communication-shape)
MailboxClosedError on a parent send is caught and surfaced as an emitWarning so a closed parent mailbox does not abort the child's turn
sendSubagentNotificationToParent emits the formatted status update via formatSubagentNotification
matches the formatSubagentNotification source-of-truth (locked by codex-status-parity)
called only on terminal transitions so the parent's run-turn loop sees one closing event per child
resolveThreadSpawnDisabledTools returns the set of tool names children must NOT see

AgenC-specific guard: blocks AgenC-only tools (TaskCreate, SendUserMessage, EnterPlanMode and similar) that codex has no equivalent for — documented strict-parity exception called out in the closing section of the codex-v2 plan
this exception is required for runtime correctness: codex has no analogous constraint only because codex has no analogous tools
buildFilteredRegistry then strips disabled tools from the child registry and wraps remaining tools via wrapToolForChild so they pick up the cwd injection above
resolveSessionMaxAgentDepth reads max-depth from session config and falls back to a positive integer default
matches codex's depth cap on agent spawn (codex spawn_subagent rejects spawns past max_agent_depth; AgenC covers the same intent at the run-agent boundary)
depth is enforced before child Session construction so a too-deep child is rejected before bootstrap

approvedExceptions.reason: Allows TypeScript top-type annotations and Codex not-recognized error assertions; behavior rows remain required.
Allows Codex followup_task identifiers and child continuation-turn assertions; not completion-deferral language.
Allows access-control assertions that a tool remains denied; not work-stoppage language.

sourceCommitDescribe: d92c909ee4 Fix migrated hook path rewriting (#20144)
